title: TESS Scattered-Light Quality-Flag Audit
question: How do TESS quality-mask policies alter flux scatter and background behaviour near scattered-light-affected cadences?
gap: A focused audit layer; not a replacement for Lightkurve, SPOC, QLP or MAST.
novelty: A focused audit layer; not a replacement for Lightkurve, SPOC, QLP or MAST.
methodology: Per-target SPOC 2-minute light curves are loaded and their QUALITY bitmask decoded against verified TESS bit definitions (lightkurve.utils.TessQualityFlags, cross-checked against real Sector 1 and Sector 40 Data Release Notes). Flux scatter (RMS/MAD) is compared before and after applying three named mask policies (default, hard, hardest), and background flux is compared between Straylight2-flagged and unflagged cadences. A key verified finding: the 'default' mask policy does NOT include the Straylight2 (scattered-light) bit -- only 'hard'/'hardest' do -- so the mask-policy choice materially changes whether scattered-light cadences are excluded at all.
assumptions: TESS Sector 40 (not Sector 1) is used for the real sample: bit 13/Straylight2 did not exist in Sector 1's early SPOC pipeline version, verified against the real Sector 1 Data Release Notes.

Bit 12/Straylight (predicted, FFI-only) is disabled for 2-minute cadence products per the real Sector 40 DRN; bit 13/Straylight2 (SPOC-pipeline empirical, per-target) is the scattered-light indicator actually used throughout.
PDCSAP_FLUX (not SAP_FLUX) is used as the primary flux series, consistent with the SPOC pipeline's systematics-corrected product.
limitations: A focused audit layer; not a replacement for Lightkurve, SPOC, QLP or MAST.

The real deterministic 3-target sample (sorted by target_name, Sector 40) happened to contain zero Straylight2-flagged cadences for all 3 targets -- a genuine real finding, not every target in a sector shows scattered-light contamination -- so the scattered-light-specific mask-policy comparison could not be exercised on real data in this small sample; the general mask-policy exclusion mechanics (via other quality bits) were still exercised and are reported.
All targets/policies are below the configured minimum_sample_size=30, flagged not hidden in results/warnings.json.
reports/report.tex could not be compiled to PDF in this environment (no local LaTeX toolchain); only structural completeness was verified.

citation.author: Biswajit Jana
